title: type aliases
manager: Named aliases for types — `type Celsius = float` — so a complex or meaningful type gets one readable name used everywhere, and changing the underlying definition changes it in one place. Aliases are documentation the compiler keeps honest: `Celsius` and `Fahrenheit` read as different intentions even when both are float underneath.
cs: transparent type aliases; no new nominal identity (structural); improves readability and single-point-of-change.
